content: Iteration 1 failed: Implement high score persistence to a file (e.g., highscore.txt) that saves on game over when score exceeds previous high score, loads on start, and displays on screen. | Execution exceeded timeout of 120s | stage=Task Executor tool=code_generator

failed_actions: Implement high score persistence to a file (e.g., highscore.txt) that saves on game over when score exceeds previous high score, loads on start, and displays on screen.

selected_goal.title: Add high score persistence across sessions
selected_goal.rationale: Players want to track their best score even after closing the game.
acceptance_criteria: High score is saved to a file (e.g., highscore.txt) upon game over if score exceeds previous.
On game start, high score is loaded from file and displayed on screen.
High score persists across application restarts.

designed_tasks.description: Implement high score persistence to a file (e.g., highscore.txt) that saves on game over when score exceeds previous high score, loads on start, and displays on screen.
acceptance_criteria: High score is saved to highscore.txt upon game over if current score exceeds the stored high score.
On game start, high score is loaded from highscore.txt and displayed on screen.
High score persists across application restarts.
High score display updates immediately after a new high score is achieved.
risk_notes: Ensure file write/read permissions are handled gracefully.
Avoid race conditions if the game is closed abruptly; consider using buffered writes.
Handle missing or corrupt highscore.txt file by initializing high score to 0.

content: Iteration 3 failed: Modify the key event handler in main.py to support WASD keys (both uppercase and lowercase) for snake movement: W up, A left, S down, D right. Ensure opposite direction is ignored and arrow keys continue to work. | Execution exceeded timeout of 120s | stage=Task Executor tool=code_generator

failed_actions: Modify the key event handler in main.py to support WASD keys (both uppercase and lowercase) for snake movement: W up, A left, S down, D right. Ensure opposite direction is ignored and arrow keys continue to work.

selected_goal.title: Add WASD key support for snake movement
selected_goal.rationale: Enhance user control options and make the game more accessible to players familiar with WASD layout.
acceptance_criteria: Pressing W, A, S, D keys (both uppercase and lowercase) moves the snake up, left, down, right respectively.
Pressing a direction opposite to the current direction is ignored to prevent instant death.
Arrow keys continue to work as before.
The game does not crash or glitch when WASD keys are pressed during gameplay or pause.

designed_tasks.description: Modify the key event handler in main.py to support WASD keys (both uppercase and lowercase) for snake movement: W up, A left, S down, D right. Ensure opposite direction is ignored and arrow keys continue to work.
acceptance_criteria: Pressing W, A, S, D keys (both uppercase and lowercase) moves the snake up, left, down, right respectively.
Pressing a direction opposite to the current direction is ignored to prevent instant death.
Arrow keys continue to work as before.
The game does not crash or glitch when WASD keys are pressed during gameplay or pause.
risk_notes: Ensure event.keysym or event.char is used correctly to capture both cases.
Test that the new key mappings do not interfere with existing pause or other key bindings.

content: Iteration 1 failed: Replace recursive restart in main() with an iterative while loop. The main game loop should be nested inside an outer while loop that, upon pressing 'R', resets game state and continues the inner loop instead of recursively calling main(). | code_generator timed out after 120s; compact retry attempted: yes | stage=Task Executor tool=code_generator

metadata.failure_reason: code_generator timed out after 120s; compact retry attempted: yes
failed_actions: Replace recursive restart in main() with an iterative while loop. The main game loop should be nested inside an outer while loop that, upon pressing 'R', resets game state and continues the inner loop instead of recursively calling main().

selected_goal.title: Refactor game restart to use iterative loop
selected_goal.rationale: Recursive restart can cause stack overflow after many restarts; using an iterative while loop is safer and more efficient.
acceptance_criteria: The main game loop uses a while loop to handle restart instead of recursive calls to main().
Restart functionality works without causing infinite recursion or stack overflow.

designed_tasks.description: Replace recursive restart in main() with an iterative while loop. The main game loop should be nested inside an outer while loop that, upon pressing 'R', resets game state and continues the inner loop instead of recursively calling main().
acceptance_criteria: The main game loop uses a while loop to handle restart instead of recursive calls to main().
Restart functionality works without causing infinite recursion or stack overflow.
Pressing 'R' during the game resets the snake, food, score, and direction to initial values and continues the game.
risk_notes: Ensure proper reset of all game variables (snake, direction, food, score) on restart; otherwise the game may start in an inconsistent state.
Avoid infinite loops by providing a way to exit the outer loop (e.g., pressing 'Q' to quit entirely).

content: Iteration 1 failed: Dynamically fetch terminal dimensions each iteration and update game boundaries in main game loop. | code_generator timed out after 300s; compact retry attempted: yes | stage=Task Executor tool=code_generator

metadata.failure_reason: code_generator timed out after 300s; compact retry attempted: yes
failed_actions: Dynamically fetch terminal dimensions each iteration and update game boundaries in main game loop.

selected_goal.title: Handle terminal window resize during gameplay
selected_goal.rationale: Currently the game does not adapt to terminal window size changes, which can cause coordinate out-of-bounds or display corruption. This is critical for a smooth user experience across different terminal configurations.
acceptance_criteria: In each game loop iteration, the current terminal dimensions are fetched using curses.getmaxyx()
Boundaries for snake movement and food generation are recalculated based on the new dimensions
The game does not crash or show visual artifacts when the terminal is resized
Score and other UI elements are re-drawn correctly after resize

designed_tasks.description: Dynamically fetch terminal dimensions each iteration and update game boundaries in main game loop.
acceptance_criteria: In each game loop iteration, before any logic or drawing, call stdscr.getmaxyx() to get current height and width.
Replace all references to initial height, width (e.g., snake start, food placement, collision checks) with the updated dimensions.
Rebuild the playable area boundaries (e.g., border) based on new dimensions.
The game continues without crash or segmentation fault when terminal is resized.
risk_notes: Resizing to extremely small sizes (e.g., less than 5 rows or columns) may require pausing or displaying a message to avoid unplayable state.
The snake may temporarily be outside new boundaries; further handling is needed in task_2.

failed_actions: Add pause/resume functionality with a visual 'PAUSED' overlay when pressing the 'P' key during active gameplay. Pause freezes snake movement, resume restarts it. Pause is disallowed when game over, and game state remains unchanged.

selected_goal.title: Add pause/resume with visual overlay
selected_goal.rationale: Players need to pause the game without losing progress; a visual overlay provides clear state feedback.
acceptance_criteria: Pressing 'P' during active gameplay freezes the snake movement and shows 'PAUSED' text centered on the canvas.
Pressing 'P' again resumes the game, hiding the overlay and restarting movement.
Pause is not allowed when the game is over.
Pause/resume does not reset the game state.

designed_tasks.description: Add pause/resume functionality with a visual 'PAUSED' overlay when pressing the 'P' key during active gameplay. Pause freezes snake movement, resume restarts it. Pause is disallowed when game over, and game state remains unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: Pressing 'P' during active gameplay freezes the snake movement and shows 'PAUSED' text centered on the canvas.
Pressing 'P' again resumes the game, hiding the overlay and restarting movement.
Pause is not allowed when the game is over.
Pause/resume does not reset the game state.
risk_notes: Ensure that pausing does not interfere with other key bindings (e.g., arrow keys).
Timer resumption must keep original interval; avoid drift.
Overlay must not prevent game over or score updates from rendering when paused.

content: Iteration 4 failed: Rewrite main.py using pygame to replace the Tkinter implementation, preserving all existing game states, controls, scoring, speed progression, and pause/restart/quit functionality. | Project validation failed with 1 blocking issue(s). | stage=Modification Evaluator tool=unknown

metadata.failure_reason: Hard validation did not pass after modification.; Acceptance criteria reviewed: pygame version launches and is fully playable with keyboard controls (arrow keys, P, R, Q/Esc); All existing states (start screen, playing, paused, game over) are visually and functionally replicated; Score display updates in real time and matches previous behavior; Snake speed increases with score (decrease interval by 10ms every 5 points, minimum 50ms)
failed_actions: Rewrite main.py using pygame to replace the Tkinter implementation, preserving all existing game states, controls, scoring, speed progression, and pause/restart/quit functionality.

selected_goal.title: Migrate snake game from Tkinter to pygame
selected_goal.rationale: Improve product fit by using the preferred game stack (pygame) and enable richer features like sound and performance.
acceptance_criteria: pygame version launches and is fully playable with keyboard controls (arrow keys, P, R, Q/Esc)
All existing states (start screen, playing, paused, game over) are visually and functionally replicated
Score display updates in real time and matches previous behavior
Snake speed increases with score (preserving existing speed progression: decrease interval by 10ms every 5 points, minimum 50ms)
Pause, restart, and quit controls work identically to the Tkinter version
The previous Tkinter implementation is replaced by the pygame version (no Tkinter code remains in the main entry point)

designed_tasks.description: Rewrite main.py using pygame to replace the Tkinter implementation, preserving all existing game states, controls, scoring, speed progression, and pause/restart/quit functionality.
acceptance_criteria: pygame version launches and is fully playable with keyboard controls (arrow keys, P, R, Q/Esc)
All existing states (start screen, playing, paused, game over) are visually and functionally replicated
Score display updates in real time and matches previous behavior
Snake speed increases with score (decrease interval by 10ms every 5 points, minimum 50ms)
Pause, restart, and quit controls work identically to the Tkinter version
The previous Tkinter implementation is replaced by the pygame version (no Tkinter code remains in main.py)
risk_notes: Requires pygame installation; update README with pygame dependency and install instructions
